artifacts.notes: BOOTSTRAPPING PARADOX — commit omitted intentionally. This prompt introduces the commit-tracking protocol; committing its own output would require the protocol to be already stable and the workpack tooling to support self-referential SHA resolution. Because this workpack is the origin of that convention, it cannot comply with it before the protocol is finalised and validated in a subsequent cycle. commit_shas will be populated in the follow-up V2_bugfix_verify or next integration gate after this workpack is merged.

handoff.summary: Commit-audit alignment completed for A0-A5. SHA1 (70434b2) is verified on feature/workpack-protocol-evolution and covers all declared change_details with one minor discrepancy in A4 (validate_templates.py absent from SHA1 stat, non-blocking). All A-series outputs now carry a real commit SHA. A5 branch_verified is true. B2 itself cannot self-commit due to a bootstrapping paradox: the commit-tracking protocol was introduced by this workpack and the tooling does not yet support self-referential SHA resolution pre-merge. B2 commit_shas will be filled in V2_bugfix_verify or the next integration gate after merge.
next_steps: Execute B1_changelog_2_2_0 if not already resolved (CHANGELOG.md needs a [2.2.0] entry).
Run V2_bugfix_verify (or re-run A5 gate) after B1 is completed to re-authorise merge.
After merge to main, backfill B2 commit_shas and set branch_verified=true in a maintenance commit.
